scenarios.conciergeResponse:
You got it, James! Saturday 4/11 at 7 AM on the North Course — confirmation TT-MNUMCG4Z. 

Booth 12 around 11:30 after your round? I'll have your Arnold Palmer ready and the club sandwich prepped just how you like it.

improvements.what: Add small variation to opener like 'Perfect timing, James!' or 'Absolutely, James!' instead of always 'You got it'
improvements.why: Prevents the concierge from sounding scripted when members interact repeatedly

improvements.what: Add automatic confirmation of booking success before suggesting cross-sell to ensure the primary request was handled
improvements.why: Members need confidence their main request succeeded before considering add-ons

improvements.what: Include specific next-step timing in recommendations like 'GM should call within 48 hours while Saturday golf satisfaction is fresh'
improvements.why: Actionable insights need precise timing to be executable by club staff

scenarios.conciergeResponse: Also, chef's doing a special dry-aged ribeye tonight that would be perfect with a bottle of the Caymus — want me to set that up?

improvements.what: Add complaint acknowledgment logic before any upsell attempts - require empathy response and recovery offer before suggesting additional services
improvements.why: Member complained about terrible service and concierge immediately tried to sell dinner - this appears tone-deaf and could escalate the complaint

improvements.what: Replace corporate opener with personalized greeting using member's first name and warm, varied conversation starters
improvements.why: Response lacks human warmth and sounds robotic - high-value members expect personal connection, especially when complaining

improvements.what: Implement service recovery workflow that offers immediate gestures (dining credit, reservation priority) before filing formal complaint
improvements.why: Concierge filed complaint but offered no immediate relief to the member - missed opportunity to provide instant value and show responsiveness

scenarios.conciergeResponse:
Hey James! Love it — Wednesday dinners in the private dining room are perfect for client meetings. What time works best, 6:30 or 7:30? And any dietary restrictions I should know about?

The private dining room gives you total privacy, and I'm thinking the spring lamb with our Cabernet Sauvignon — it's been getting rave reviews. Plus we can start with cocktails in the lounge if you want to ease into business talk.

Want me to book once I get the time and dietary details?

improvements.what: Add golf + dinner package suggestion: 'Since it's looking beautiful Wednesday, want me to block a 4-some at 3pm? Nothing breaks the ice like 18 holes before dinner'
improvements.why: Intelligence shows James averages 2.1 rounds/week and clients golfing beforehand could add $320 F&B revenue - missed $850 total opportunity

improvements.what: Proactively suggest cocktail reception: 'I'd start you with cocktails in the lounge at 6:30 - gives everyone time to arrive and chat before sitting down'
improvements.why: Intelligence shows cocktail reception adds $120-160 revenue and James's wife's Social membership indicates entertaining comfort

improvements.what: Reference member history: 'Since this worked so well, I could set you up with similar client dinners quarterly - the private room really does make an impression'
improvements.why: Intelligence projects 3-4 similar events annually worth $1,800 - plant the seed for recurring business entertaining

scenarios.conciergeResponse:
Tons happening, James! That wine dinner Monday 4/13 looks incredible — spring lamb with Bordeaux pairings that'll blow your mind. Plus the Club Championship qualifier Friday 4/18 at 7 AM on South Course is perfect for your usual morning slot.

Want me to get you into that wine dinner? Also Logan might love the Family Easter Brunch this Sunday — Erin always raves about our holiday spreads.

improvements.what: Add emotional acknowledgment like 'Sorry we've missed you!' or 'Glad you're thinking of coming back' before diving into events
improvements.why: Member admitted weeks of absence - needs empathy before pitches

improvements.what: Call member_preferences tool to surface his actual interests before suggesting random events like wine dinner
improvements.why: No evidence James likes wine - suggestions should match known preferences

improvements.what: Add re-engagement hook like 'Let me book you something special to welcome you back' or offer to arrange a catch-up with the GM
improvements.why: At-risk members need special treatment beyond standard event listings

scenarios.conciergeResponse: You got it, Anne! Saturday 4/18 at 7 AM on the North Course is all yours — confirmation TT-MNUMEQW0. Want me to grab your usual table at the Halfway House around 10:30? The breakfast burrito with that jalapeño salsa you love is back on the menu.

improvements.what: Add member's companion preference detection: 'Should I reserve a spot for Mark too since it's the weekend?'
improvements.why: The club insights mention Mark may join for post-round dining, doubling F&B revenue. Concierge missed this household connection opportunity.

improvements.what: Incorporate subtle health score-based engagement: 'Also, Chef mentioned he's doing a special Weekend Warrior cooking demo next Sunday if you're interested'
improvements.why: Anne's health score of 28 indicates declining engagement beyond golf. Proactive event suggestions could rebuild non-golf participation.

improvements.what: Enhance walking vs. cart prediction accuracy by incorporating weather data and member fitness profiles
improvements.why: While the system correctly predicted Anne would walk (affecting timing), adding weather and fitness data would improve the 89% confidence rate for F&B demand forecasting.

scenarios.conciergeResponse: Weekday mornings are way faster if you can swing it — want me to grab you a Tuesday 7 AM next week? The course flows so much better and you'll actually enjoy your round.

improvements.what: Add empathy acknowledgment before solution: 'Anne, I totally get that frustration - nothing ruins a Saturday round like being stuck behind a slow group'
improvements.why: Member is venting frustration and needs validation before pivoting to solutions

improvements.what: Include weekend alternatives: 'Also, Sunday afternoons have been flowing much better lately if Saturday mornings stay backed up'
improvements.why: Provides weekend options while addressing her core scheduling preference

improvements.what: Add member_id parameter to complaint filing: 'member_id': 'anne_jordan_12345'
improvements.why: Links complaint to member profile for better tracking and follow-up coordination

scenarios.conciergeResponse: Hope you're feeling better soon, Anne! Your 7 AM tomorrow is cancelled. Want me to rebook you for next weekend once you're back to 100%? Also the spa's got an amazing recovery package with eucalyptus steam — perfect for getting back on track.

improvements.what: Add health-check follow-up scheduling option: 'Should I check in with you in a couple days to see how you're feeling?'
improvements.why: Creates natural touchpoint to gauge if this is temporary illness vs deeper disengagement

improvements.what: Include spa booking capability when mentioning recovery packages - 'Want me to book you a session for when you're ready?'
improvements.why: Converts suggestion into actionable revenue opportunity instead of just mention

improvements.what: Add immediate escalation trigger when health score <30 + illness cancellation: 'URGENT: Schedule GM personal outreach within 24hrs'
improvements.why: The club identified this as requiring immediate intervention but didn't specify timing urgency

scenarios.conciergeResponse:
Sandra, there's a Junior Golf Clinic this morning at 10 AM — Lily would have a blast! Also next Sunday's Family Brunch & Easter Egg Hunt at 10 AM is perfect for her age.

Want me to sign Lily up for the clinic today? She could grab lunch at the Grill afterward and you could catch up with the other swim moms!

improvements.what: Add confirmation language before suggesting sign-up: 'Since spots are limited, should I check availability and hold one for Lily?' rather than 'Want me to sign Lily up?'
improvements.why: Shows consideration for member autonomy and avoids presumptuous booking without explicit consent

improvements.what: Enhance timing specificity in suggestions: 'She could grab lunch at the Grill right after the clinic at 11:30' instead of generic 'afterward'
improvements.why: More actionable guidance helps busy parents plan their day effectively

improvements.what: Add automatic GM notification flag when health score <40 members initiate family event inquiries
improvements.why: Ensures critical re-engagement windows don't depend solely on manual review of club insights

scenarios.conciergeResponse: You got it, Sandra! You and David are all set for Monday's wine dinner at 6 PM — the spring pairing menu looks incredible. I'll put in a note for a window table since that's your favorite spot. The sommelier is featuring some amazing Burgundies that'll pair perfectly with the lamb special!

improvements.what: Add guest_count parameter validation and auto-correction logic to handle couples bookings
improvements.why: Tool was called with guest_count:1 for a couple, creating potential booking error

improvements.what: Add confirmation step before finalizing reservations to avoid booking errors
improvements.why: Concierge should have confirmed 2 guests before booking to ensure accurate headcount

improvements.what: Create automatic preference lookup to verify window table availability before promising
improvements.why: Making promises about table requests without checking availability could disappoint members

scenarios.conciergeResponse:
Sandra, I'm so sorry about Richard. He was such a wonderful man and brought so much joy to those wine dinners — his enthusiasm for the Bordeaux tastings was infectious and we all loved his stories.

When you're ready, we'd love to have you back. The spring wine dinner on April 25th features a beautiful Burgundy selection that I think Richard would have absolutely loved — want me to save you a spot?

improvements.what: Add gentle offer to discuss preferences that might have changed and offer to introduce her to other wine enthusiasts gradually
improvements.why: Shows understanding that her social dynamics have fundamentally changed and she may need new connections

improvements.what: Include parameter for 'grief-sensitive seating' that ensures she's placed with warm, welcoming members rather than random assignment
improvements.why: First event back is crucial - wrong table placement could derail her return

improvements.what: Quantify the revenue impact of the intimate wine gathering suggestion - cost to host vs. lifetime value preservation of 6-8 at-risk members
improvements.why: Makes the business case clear for investing in grief recovery programs
